violations.text: The door to the outside, located in the (cafeteria ), is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/04/2025

The presence of (droppings ) is not controlled as evidenced by (kitchen storage area and food storage shelves ). The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/18/2026

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the (kitchen) area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/04/2025

The floors in kitchen (warewashing area) have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/04/2025

The door to the outside (A-2), located in the cafeteria, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/18/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the kitchen area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/04/2025

The floor in the dry storage area has an accumulation of soil residue, droppings, and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Establishment is to clean any and all droppings using appropriate means of sanitization. Establishment is to increase pest control if new activity is found and as frequently as needed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2025

The doors to the outside, located in the kitchen and cafeteria, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/18/2025

The presence of mice is not controlled as evidenced by droppings throughout the kitchen and dry storage. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Establishment is to clean any and all droppings using appropriate means of sanitization. Establishment is to increase pest control as frequently as needed and is to have a pest control report available for inspector at the time of next inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 11/04/2025
The walk-in cooler handle was observed in poor repair with tape covering handle. Equipment must be in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/11/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the kitchen area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 11/04/2025

The floors in the kitchen and dry storage area have an accumulation of soil residue, droppings, and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 11/04/2025

The door to the outside, located in the back kitchen and cafeteria areas, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/23/2024
Door knob located in the walk-in cooler was not in good repair (taped). New Violation. Correct By: 03/20/2025

The following non-food contact surfaces have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: - hotel pans, - utensil containers, - shelving units under the service line, - cart shelf - pizza pan racks. New Violation. Correct By: 03/06/2025
The ceiling in the (kitchen) area is covered with tiles that are not smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/23/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the kitchen area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2025

The floors in the back storage areas have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/06/2025

The door to the outside, located in the (back kitchen area), is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/23/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the (walk in cooler fans ) have an accumulation of dust. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/23/2024
The ceiling in the (kitchen) area is covered with tiles that are not smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. New Violation. Correct By: 10/23/2024

The floors in food storage rooms have an accumulation of soil residue, food debris, rodent droppings, and/or other debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 09/23/2024
The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site Certified Food Safety Manager registered with RIDOH. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/27/2023

Chicken fingers observed held at 122-128 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2024

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/02/2023

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings on shelves in dry storage. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the fan guards in the walk-in and free-standing fan in the kitchen have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2024

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/27/2023
Inspector observed rodenticide on dry storage shelving. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 10/26/2023

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen (back door), is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 11/02/2023

The dishwasher is a temperature sanitizer. The dishwasher rinse cycle was only reaching 140 degrees F. Until the machine is fixed, the dishwasher can be used to wash pots, pans and utensils but must be sanitized in the 3 bay sink. Maintenance has been called for service. New Violation. Correct By: 11/02/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the hood has an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 11/02/2023

Inspector observed the following areas to have an accumulation of soil residue, rodent droppings and food debris - Shelving (dry storage) - Floors (dry storage) The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 11/02/2023

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/28/2023
